intro: Iconify and Phosphor Icons land in similar territory on the hard numbers, so the decision comes down to fit with your workflow rather than a headline metric. Iconify is built for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually and is not a fit for tiny static sites or teams that only ever use one curated icon set. Phosphor Icons is built for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products and is not a fit for ultra-minimal uis or projects where single-weight consistency is the goal. On the scoring side, Iconify rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Phosphor Icons rates 5/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Iconify you accept that runtime lookup adds a small performance cost, while Phosphor Icons comes with the reality that larger catalog than needed for simple uis. Neither side is free.
quick_take: Iconify is for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually; Phosphor Icons is for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter.
verdict_a: Choose Iconify if you are projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually.
verdict_b: Choose Phosphor Icons if you are brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products.

faq.q: Is Iconify cheaper than Phosphor Icons?
faq.a: Both list a free pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Iconify to Phosphor Icons?
faq.a: Iconify has low lock-in (open-source icon framework); Phosphor Icons has low lock-in (open source icon library, free to use). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Iconify; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Iconify and Phosphor Icons score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Phosphor Icons a good alternative to Iconify?
faq.a: Phosphor Icons is built for brands wanting expressive weight variation across web, mobile, and flutter products and explicitly not for ultra-minimal uis or projects where single-weight consistency is the goal, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Iconify for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
